officialSources.title: Allow only certain external connections for Apps Script and Sheets
evidence: Google Workspace admins can allowlist external domains for Apps Script and Sheets URL access.
The same help page says Apps Script scripts and Sheets functions can send or fetch data using any URL by default.

officialSources.title: Control which apps access Google Workspace data
evidence: Google Workspace admins can mark OAuth apps as Trusted, Specific Google data, Limited, or Blocked.
The admin control surface is tenant policy, not a per-workbook artifact checked into this repository.

officialSources.title: Choose Google Sheets API scopes
evidence: Google documents Sheets API scopes at spreadsheet-file scope and recommends choosing narrow scopes.
The same page says ProtectedRange is used to prevent editing of sheet cells or ranges.

officialSources.title: Protect, hide and edit sheets
evidence: Google documents protected ranges as an edit-control feature and warns not to treat it as a security measure.
Google documents that protected-sheet viewers or editors can still copy, print, import, or export data depending on their access.

officialSources.title: Get started with encrypted files in Drive, Docs, Sheets and Slides
evidence: Google Workspace client-side encryption can cover Sheets files when enabled by an admin.
Google documents feature limits for encrypted Sheets, including static or removed features such as external data functions, protected ranges, Apps Script, and add-ons.

officialSources.title: Change macro security settings in Excel
evidence: Microsoft documents Excel macro security settings in Trust Center.
Microsoft documents that organization policy can prevent users from changing macro security settings.

officialSources.title: Working with Excel 4.0 macros
evidence: Microsoft documents that Excel still supports Excel 4.0 macros.
Microsoft recommends against enabling all macros and flags that potentially dangerous code can run.

officialSources.title: What is Protected View?
evidence: Microsoft documents Protected View for files from the internet, unsafe locations, Outlook attachments, file block, and validation failures.
Microsoft documents Excel-specific Protected View settings for untrusted text and database files.

officialSources.title: Block or unblock external content in Office documents
evidence: Microsoft documents Trust Center warnings and controls for Excel external data connections, workbook links, linked data types, DDE, and Microsoft Query files.
Microsoft documents that external data connections can access or change data with the user's credentials if enabled.

officialSources.title: Protection and security in Excel
evidence: Microsoft documents file-level encryption, read-only access, IRM restrictions, and digital signatures for Excel files.
Microsoft documents that worksheet-level protection is not intended as a security feature.

officialSources.title: Apply sensitivity labels to your files
evidence: Microsoft documents sensitivity labels for Excel and other Office files.
Microsoft documents that labels can apply encryption and user-defined permissions depending on organization configuration.

limitations: This artifact captures current official public documentation, not a live tenant audit of Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 policy state.
The comparison proves direct external security evidence exists; it does not claim the whole product is 10x better across every category.

dimensions.title: Workbook Code Execution
biligEvidence: The generated security posture scorecard scans formula/runtime TypeScript files for dynamic code execution and process-spawn imports.
The XLSX import control imports macro-enabled bytes with a VBA payload and verifies macros are ignored instead of executed or exported.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google Sheets automation is governed through Apps Script, API scopes, app access controls, and optional URL allowlisting.
Google Sheets API scopes are spreadsheet-file scoped; protected ranges are separate edit controls.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Excel supports VBA macros and Excel 4.0 macros under Trust Center macro settings.
Microsoft warns against enabling all macros and documents organization policy controls for macro settings.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig's checked workbook formula/import path is non-code-executing by construction; Sheets and Excel expose broader script or macro systems governed by tenant and Trust Center controls.
residualLimitations: bilig still needs broader third-party security review before a blanket security leadership claim.
Google and Microsoft tenant policy hardening can materially change the incumbent posture.

dimensions.title: External Network Egress
biligEvidence: The browser CSP control verifies explicit connect-src origins for the workbook runtime.
The deployment NetworkPolicy evidence restricts runtime ingress to documented app, Zero, Alloy, and Traefik peers.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google documents an admin URL allowlist for Apps Script and Sheets external URL access.
Google documents that Apps Script and Sheets URL access is broad by default unless the admin allowlist is configured.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Excel Trust Center can prompt or disable external data connections, workbook links, linked data types, DDE, and Microsoft Query files.
Excel Protected View covers files from internet, unsafe locations, Outlook attachments, and validation failures.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig has checked repo-level network allowlists for the shipped runtime; Sheets and Excel expose policy surfaces for external connections but require tenant or client configuration.
residualLimitations: This comparison does not probe live Google or Microsoft tenant policies.
bilig outbound service-level egress policy beyond the browser/runtime evidence remains a future hardening lane.

dimensions.title: Workbook Protection Boundaries
biligEvidence: The generated security posture scorecard keeps workbook-agent shared medium and high-risk bundles behind owner review.
The auditability scorecard separately proves preview, authoritative apply, undo, and revert controls for sensitive workbook changes.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google documents protected sheets and ranges as editing controls and warns they should not be used as a security measure.
Google documents that users with sufficient access can copy or export protected spreadsheet data.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Microsoft documents worksheet protection as a cell-modification control, not a security feature.
Microsoft documents separate file-level encryption and rights-management options for stronger Excel file protection.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig distinguishes workflow approval/audit controls from file confidentiality; Google and Microsoft official docs make a similar distinction for sheet or worksheet protection.
residualLimitations: bilig still needs full role-based permission matrix evidence before this dimension can become a product-wide lead claim.
The comparison is scoped to documented controls, not red-team attempts to bypass protection models.

dimensions.title: Enterprise Data Protection
biligEvidence: bilig has checked dependency audit, runtime package hardening, CSP, and deployment NetworkPolicy controls in the security posture scorecard.
bilig does not yet claim parity with Google Workspace client-side encryption or Microsoft Purview sensitivity-label ecosystems.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google Workspace client-side encryption can cover Sheets files when enabled by an admin.
Google documents meaningful feature limits for encrypted Sheets, including Apps Script/add-ons removal and static external data functions.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Microsoft documents Excel file-level encryption, IRM restrictions, digital signatures, and sensitivity labels.
Microsoft documents that sensitivity labels can persist with files and apply encryption or permissions depending on tenant configuration.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig now has direct external comparison evidence and strong repo-local runtime controls, while incumbent enterprise encryption and labeling ecosystems remain broader than bilig's current checked evidence.
residualLimitations: This dimension remains a security evidence gap for overall product dominance, despite the external comparison now being captured.
Future work should add bilig tenant/admin policy controls or document why the product intentionally has a different deployment security model.
